name: Asana
description: Work management platform for teams to track tasks, projects, and workflows; provides an API to create, read, update, and automate Asana data and embed app components.

auth_methods.note: Asana explicitly documents OAuth 2.0 for apps and Personal Access Tokens (used as Bearer tokens) for testing and scripts; both are sent as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'.

credential_access.note: Personal Access Tokens can be created from the developer console for testing (no human approval). Creating OAuth client credentials is done via the developer console (create-an-app). Pricing page shows free plan exists and docs do not state API access requires a paid plan.

api_surface.note: The Asana API is RESTful (base URL https://app.asana.com/api/1.0) and documents endpoints for tasks, projects, users, teams, webhooks, attachments, custom fields, etc., covering most product objects.

buildability.note: Public REST API docs plus self-serve personal access tokens and app registration in the developer console allow building integrations today.

queries: Asana developer API documentation root developers.asana.com api docs Asana API developers.asana.com OAuth personal access token developer documentation
site:developers.asana.com register app "Register an app" Asana
Asana API requires paid plan partner "API access requires" site:asana.com OR site:developers.asana.com "API" "paid"
